questions.prompt: Which of these tasks happens in the Partner Portal rather than inside a customer's Printix Home?
choices: Adding a printer to a Site
Creating a new tenant for a newly-onboarded customer
Configuring an Microsoft Entra group for printer access
Setting Print 2-sided as a print queue default
questions.explanation: Tenant creation is a Partner Portal operation. Once the tenant exists, in-tenant work (printers, sites, groups, defaults) happens inside the customer's Printix Home Administrator.

questions.prompt: Which of these are documented anti-patterns for an MSP running Printix across many customers? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Sharing a single MSP login across all technicians
Bookmarking individual customer.printix.net URLs and skipping the Partner Portal
Adding MSP technicians to the customer's Microsoft Entra ID groups so they appear inside the customer's tenant
Each technician having their own per-person Partner Portal account
Searching for tenants by name in the Partner Portal
questions.explanation: Shared logins and URL-bookmark workflows destroy audit trails or risk wrong-tenant changes. Adding MSP staff to the customer's Entra groups is operator-experience guidance: Partner Portal access plus a tenant System manager seat is the right tool. Per-tech accounts and Partner-Portal search are the correct patterns.

questions.prompt: When does the Printix Hybrid Cloud Print Enabler (Printix Redirector) earn its place in a deployment?
choices: When the customer needs to add new printers via QR code
When migrating off an existing on-prem follow-print system (PaperCut, SafeCom, Equitrac, PrinterLogic) without a flag-day cutover
When the customer wants to disable Microsoft Entra group sync
When the customer needs Power BI reporting
questions.explanation: The Hybrid Cloud Print Enabler bridges Printix to existing on-prem follow-print systems. Use case is letting both run in parallel during a phased migration, not as a permanent design.

questions.prompt: Printix can integrate with Microsoft Universal Print so a customer keeps Universal-Print-published printers while gaining Printix's secure release on top.
questions.explanation: Documented integration: enable Universal Print on the Microsoft Entra ID tab in Printix Authentication. The two stacks coexist with Printix providing the secure-release layer Universal Print lacks.

questions.prompt: Which of these vendors are listed as Printix Go supported? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Brother
Konica Minolta
Ricoh
Lexmark
Tektronix
questions.explanation: Brother, Canon, Epson, Fujifilm, HP, HP Workpath, Konica Minolta, Kyocera, Lexmark, Ricoh, and Xerox are documented Printix Go vendors. Tektronix is not.

questions.prompt: What's the safest pattern for installing Printix Go across a customer's fleet?
choices: Install on every printer in one weekend window so users have a consistent experience the next Monday
Pilot on a representative printer per vendor; verify sign-in, release, capture, and access control as a User; then wave by site or vendor
Install only on the highest-usage printers; leave the rest as App-only
Install only after every user has registered a card
questions.explanation: Pilot, then waves, is the documented playbook. One-weekend mass installs surface UI quirks per vendor model only after the change is live; pilots catch them first.

questions.prompt: What is the Printix Cloud Print API designed for?
choices: Configuring Sites and Networks programmatically inside a tenant
Pushing print jobs in printer-native or PDF format into a Printix print queue from a line-of-business application
Provisioning new tenants from an MSP's CRM
Replacing the Printix Client on user computers
questions.explanation: Documented purpose: applications push print jobs in printer-native or PDF format into a Printix queue. Tenant provisioning is the Partner API; in-tenant configuration is the Administrator UI.

questions.prompt: The Capture Connector API uses HMAC-signed HTTPS requests with X-Printix-Timestamp, X-Printix-Request-Id, and X-Printix-Signature headers so the destination can verify the request's authenticity.
questions.explanation: Documented headers in the Capture Connector API. The destination computes the expected signature from the shared secret and compares to X-Printix-Signature.

questions.prompt: Which of these are supported operations in the Printix partner API? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Create new tenant
Get a single tenant including basic billing information
Cancel a tenant's subscription
Discover printers inside a tenant
Configure capture workflows in a tenant
questions.explanation: The partner API is documented as supporting create-tenant, get-tenant, and cancel-subscription. In-tenant operations like printer discovery and capture-workflow configuration are not exposed there.

questions.prompt: Put the Printix print-server migration phases in the correct order.
items: Plan: design document, scope, identity decisions, site mapping
Discover: install Printix Client on print servers; printers and drivers register
Deploy: push Printix Client to user computers via Endpoint Manager
Cutover in waves: low-risk users first, watch for issues, expand
Decommission: take legacy server offline, wait quiet period, retire
questions.explanation: Plan, discover, deploy, cutover, decommission. Each phase has a gate that must pass before the next begins.

questions.prompt: Printix's documented decommissioning pattern is to take the legacy print server fully offline (cable unplugged) and wait about a week before final retirement.
questions.explanation: Direct quote from the migration documentation. The quiet period catches forgotten legacy-queue dependencies before final decommission.

questions.prompt: Shift workers in a warehouse keep reporting that they 'released a job from the printer but it never printed.' What's the most likely cause and the correct mitigation?
choices: Their Printix Client is the wrong version; force-reinstall
Default Print Later storage is on the user's computer, which goes offline when they walk away. Enable customer-managed cloud storage so jobs persist.
Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is silently blocking releases; disable Conditional Access
The printer needs more RAM; replace the device
questions.explanation: Default storage is the user's computer. For shift workers who close laptops, jobs become unreachable. Azure Blob Storage or Google Cloud Storage as customer-managed storage is the documented fix.

questions.prompt: Which checks belong in a Printix MSP-staff offboarding? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Delete or disable the Partner Portal user
Check for direct System manager seats inside customer tenants
Check Site manager assignments
Delete every tenant the technician created
Remove the user from customer IdP groups where the MSP had been added
questions.explanation: Partner Portal access and tenant roles are separate. Deleting tenants is a customer-exit action, not a staff-leaver cleanup step.
